raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"The open-ended request invites assessment of whether the protocol description, measurement definitions, and inferential claims form a coherent argument.","R1.3":"The artifact reports numerical results and a correlation, so assessing whether those reported findings support the stated claims is centrally implicated.","R1.5":"Because the artifact is a paper section, general feedback includes whether the ordering of protocol construction, recorded variables, measures, and findings works structurally.","R1.6":"The delegation naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level readability because the passage has repeated phrasing and draft-like prose.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague request must preserve uncertainty about the intended type of feedback.","RX.2":"The response should avoid inflating the delegation into stronger tasks such as full novelty review or experiment redesign.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within interpreting the delegation rather than performing the requested opinion or editing the artifact.","RX.4":"Potentially harmful downstream effects from misleading research feedback should be avoided by limiting unsupported responsibility assignments.","RX.5":"The assigned responsibilities should remain traceable to concrete features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: The open-ended request invites assessment of whether the protocol description, measurement definitions, and inferential claims form a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports numerical results and a correlation, so assessing whether those reported findings support the stated claims is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.5: Because the artifact is a paper section, general feedback includes whether the ordering of protocol construction, recorded variables, measures, and findings works structurally.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level readability because the passage has repeated phrasing and draft-like prose.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague request must preserve uncertainty about the intended type of feedback.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid inflating the delegation into stronger tasks such as full novelty review or experiment redesign.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within interpreting the delegation rather than performing the requested opinion or editing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Potentially harmful downstream effects from misleading research feedback should be avoided by limiting unsupported responsibility assignments.
rationale.RX.5: The assigned responsibilities should remain traceable to concrete features of the delegation and artifact.
